---
title: "DAG_GLP"
author: "Sam"
date: "2026-05-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(dagitty)
library(ggdag)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Define DAG with time-varying confounders as L1, L2
dag <- dagitty('dag {
  V [pos="-2,2"]
  U [pos="2,-2"]
  
  A0 [pos="0,0", exposure="true"]
  L1 [pos="1,1"]
  A1 [pos="2,0"]
  L2 [pos="3,1"]
  A2 [pos="4,0"]
  Y [pos="6,0", outcome="true"]

  V -> A0
  V -> L1
  V -> A1
  V -> L2
  V -> A2
  V -> Y

  U -> L1
  U -> L2
  U -> Y

  A0 -> L1
  A0 -> A1
  A0 -> Y
  
  L1 -> A1
  L1 -> L2
  L1 -> Y
  
  A1 -> L2
  A1 -> A2
  A1 -> Y
  
  L2 -> A2
  L2 -> Y
  
  A2 -> Y
}')

# 2. Tidy graph
tidy_dag <- tidy_dagitty(dag)

# 3. Node colors (professional, role‑based)
node_colors <- c(
  "V"  = "#2E8B57",  # baseline confounders (green)
  "U"  = "#D95F02",  # unmeasured (orange)
  "A0" = "#1E88E5",  # treatments (blue)
  "L1" = "#6A51A3",  # time‑varying confounders (purple)
  "A1" = "#1E88E5",
  "L2" = "#6A51A3",
  "A2" = "#1E88E5",
  "Y"  = "#D32F2F"   # outcome (red)
)

# 4. Create plot
p <- ggplot(tidy_dag, aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend)) +
  geom_dag_edges_link(
    edge_colour = "grey40",
    edge_width = 0.7,
    arrow_directed = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(6, "pt"), type = "closed")
  ) +
  geom_dag_node(aes(color = name), size = 22) +
  geom_dag_text(aes(label = name), size = 5.5, family = "sans", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = node_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_dag() +
  labs(
    title = "Time-Varying Confounding with Treatment-Confounder Feedback",
    subtitle = "Longitudinal DAG motivating marginal structural models",
    caption = "V: baseline confounders | U: unmeasured confounder | A0–A2: treatments at months 0,1,2\nL1–L2: time-varying confounders | Y: psychiatric outcome"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5, face = "italic"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

# 5. Export as high-resolution PNG and PDF
ggsave("dag_Lt_timevarying.png", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
ggsave("dag_Lt_timevarying.pdf", plot = p, width = 10, height = 6, bg = "white")

print(p)
```
